title: Powers of powers
description: Rewrite powers of powers. For example, (x²)⁵ can be written as x¹⁰.
topic_path: Get ready for exponents, radicals, & irrational numbers

hints: The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.
The general rule for powers of powers is $\left(x^m\right)^{n}=x^{m\cdot n}$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $4^n$.*

$\left(4^2\right)^{4}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: In conclusion, $\left(4^2\right)^{4}=4^8$.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Simplify**.  
*Rewrite the expression in the form $9^n$.*

$\left(9^2\right)^{5}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: In conclusion, $\left(9^2\right)^{5}=9^{10}$.

$\left(5^3\right)^{2}=$ [[☃ expression 1]]
hints: In conclusion, $\left(5^3\right)^{2}=5^{6}$.
